Juncus nutans, also known as Sharp's Rush or Nuttall's Rush Rush, is a type of flowering plant in the rushes family (Juncaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, providing various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Juncus nutans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Juncus nutans is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants, to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and coughs.
